name: Perplexity Computer
description: Perplexity's computer-use agents autonomously browse the web, extract structured data, fill forms, and complete multi-step research workflows. Leverages Perplexity's web indexing and citation-grounded search for agents that navigate live websites and return structured outputs with source attribution.
oneLiner: AI-powered computer use and web data extraction agents with citation-grounded research capabilities.
pricing: Pro $20/mo; Enterprise custom pricing for API and computer-use agents

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: NL prompts or API calls define research tasks. Agent browses, extracts, compiles results. Prompt-first with minimal config.
strengths: Massive user base for feedback loops
notableFeatures: Computer-use agents for live website interaction
Hybrid search combining index + real-time browsing

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: NL prompting is intuitive. API for programmatic creation.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Citation trails provide visibility. Dedicated tools still early.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Solid API docs. Computer-use docs newer.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Prompt-based iteration is fast. No build steps.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Shared API keys. No version control.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Web search as context loader. No enterprise RAG.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Query and citation logs. No SOC2 confirmed for computer-use.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Search infra handles high volumes. Computer-use more resource-intensive.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Basic usage dashboards. No advanced observability.

scoringRationale: Perplexity scores 68/100. Builder (17) good NL interface. Deployment (14) solid SaaS. Ecosystem (7) strong funding but computer-use is new. Governance (10) and Collaboration (8) need enterprise maturation.
targetUser: Teams needing citation-grounded web research at scale

walkthrough.title: How Perplexity Computer Works: Citation-Grounded Web Research

steps.title: Define Your Research Task
steps.description: Describe what you need in natural language: 'Find the top 20 AI startups that raised Series A in Q1 2026, including funding amount, lead investor, and product description.' The agent plans a multi-step web research strategy.

steps.title: Agent Browses the Live Web
steps.description: Using computer-use capabilities, the agent autonomously navigates websites, search engines, and databases. It handles dynamic JavaScript pages, pagination, and login-gated content where credentials are provided.

steps.title: Extract and Structure Data
steps.description: As the agent browses, it extracts relevant data into your specified schema. Every data point is citation-grounded — linked back to the source URL and page where it was found.

steps.title: Compile Research Output
steps.description: The agent compiles findings into structured formats (JSON, markdown tables, reports) with full source attribution. Cross-references multiple sources to validate accuracy and flags conflicting information.

steps.title: API Integration for Scale
steps.description: Use the API for batch research jobs, recurring competitive intelligence monitoring, and integration into data pipelines. Enterprise tier provides higher rate limits and priority processing.
